element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary ground-based enemy damage tool; introduced immediately with basic enemy types

element_introduction_order.element: Cane pogo bounce (mid-air attack and mobility)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core vertical mobility mechanic; allows access to higher areas and bouncing across hazards
Currency system; affects ending quality; $1M per boss defeated, optional collection for wealth
Passive healing items; found in certain areas and chests throughout all levels
Tier-gated progression; one per level; requires specific learned mechanics to defeat; rewards treasures
Player chooses which of 5 destinations to visit next; creates pacing variability; some levels must be revisited

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Across multiple levels (African Mines/Transylvania required revisits)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Soft locks requiring NPC dialogue or specific inventory items; encourages backtracking
Environmental damage; requires pogo bounce precision or avoidance

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Hidden across all five destination levels
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional collectibles; two secret treasures scattered between levels; rewards exploration
Strategic money sink; enables bad ending if overused; ties wealth to survival difficulty

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Transylvania - Final area after collecting all 5 main treasures
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Climactic multi-stage encounter; demonstrates mastery of all core mechanics; triggers ending sequence

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating level bosses and collecting treasure rewards
Finding ice cream health pickups after taking damage
Reaching safe platform sections after hazard gauntlets
NPC interactions providing hints or opening new areas
Returning to level select to choose next destination (player-controlled pacing break)
typical_tension_triggers: Navigating spike/pit hazard sequences requiring precision pogo bounces
Time pressure from limited health pool between healing items
Verticality puzzles requiring multiple bounce sequences over damage zones
Mandatory backtracking through previously cleared areas with new objectives
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-controlled tension modulation through non-linear level selection; individual levels build tension through vertical gauntlets and boss encounters; relief comes via successful collection and NPC rewards; overall rhythm determined by player choice of stage order and exploration depth

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-screen areas above platforms; chests and hidden corners in background spaces; non-linear stage availability creates spatial discovery moments
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Diagonal or vertical alcoves containing treasure chests; areas requiring multi-bounce pogo sequences to access; backtracking with newly acquired items unlocks previously blocked paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: African Mines introduces core mechanics (swing, pogo, collection) with moderate platform density and basic enemy types; first boss encounter is tutorial-level threat; ice cream and diamonds abundant
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Amazon and Himalayas escalate hazard complexity with longer vertical sequences, tighter pogo timing requirements, and more aggressive enemy placements; boss patterns introduce RNG variability (Boss 2/3 RNG dependencies documented); optional secrets require skill demonstration
difficulty_curve.late_game: Transylvania and Moon levels compress hazards tightly and introduce stage-specific gimmicks; final boss gauntlet (Dracula Duck + Glomgold/DeSpell) requires sustained performance; wealth accumulation for good ending becomes active concern
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (moderate difficulty, introduces pattern recognition)
Amazon level second section (large gap requires specific pogo assist or speed boost; PAL version mitigates via increased movement speed)
Boss 2 transformation phase (RNG-dependent flight patterns create replayability difficulty)
Boss 3 rock phase (inconsistent X speed and Y speed variance adds mechanical unpredictability)
Final gauntlet sequence (Dracula Duck + two-boss consecutive fights with minimal healing opportunities)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level features ascending or descending platforming sections with increasing hazard density, culminating in a single mandatory boss encounter that must be defeated to collect the treasure (and $1M reward). Boss patterns are partially RNG-dependent, creating variance in optimal strategy.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: African Mines (introductory), Amazon (escalated difficulty), Transylvania (final revisit)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Non-linear stage select with mandatory backtracking
notable_level_patterns.description: Five destination levels can be visited in any order, but African Mines and Transylvania must be revisited to acquire essential progression items (keys, character interactions). This creates pacing control for the player while enforcing certain spatial relationships.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels participate; stage select screen appears after each boss defeat

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels begin with horizontal exploration/navigation sections containing NPCs, item pickups, and optional paths, then transition to vertical climbing/hazard sequences that gate access to the boss chamber.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: African Mines (horizontal mine entrance → vertical shaft climb to boss)

notable_level_patterns.description: Two secret treasures hidden across the five levels, requiring either off-grid exploration or specific item usage to access. These reward thorough spatial investigation and offer optional completion content.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Scattered across Amazon, Himalayas, Transylvania, Moon (exact locations not documented in sources)
